samples.prompt: Task objective: visual security for rigid body kinematics and coupling. Core scenario: forklift overspeed during a tight turn causing pallet cargo to slide outward under centrifugal force. Reference subject: veh 014. Motion requirement / viewpoint motion fidelity: perform a smooth constant-radius orbit around the subject; target motion value 45.0. Industrial logic and fact alignment check: preserve the causal chain, equipment roles, personnel or vehicle states, compliance triggers, alarms, stops, and consequences described by the scenario. Geometric integrity check: preserve topology, rigid joints, load-bearing members, local defect boundaries, repeated structures, component counts, and spatial relationships; only the requested failure or defect region may change. Physical plausibility check: obey gravity, contact, rigid-body coupling, load paths, pressure direction, fluid diffusion, heat or flame propagation, and feasible emergency dynamics. Temporal consistency check: maintain object identity, material state, background, local event state, and cause-effect continuity across all frames without flicker, melting, or role switching. Reference and motion fidelity check: execute the requested viewpoint control while locking the reference identity, perspective, non-mutated regions, and background details. Execution constraints: do not add or remove functional parts; do not change component counts; do not merge separate structures unless the requested failure requires it; do not bend rigid members; do not replace the industrial scene with a different object. Scoring emphasis: geometric_integrity=1.70, industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment=1.10, physical_plausibility=1.45, reference_and_motion_fidelity=0.85, temporal_consistency=1.25.

constraint_annotations.domain_scenario: forklift overspeed during a tight turn causing pallet cargo to slide outward under centrifugal force

samples.application_value: Rigid mechanisms, robotic arms, cranes, CNC machines, excavators, and multi-link equipment where coupled motion must stay feasible.

axis_rubric.geometric_integrity: Highest priority: links, rigid joints, axes, supports, and spatial topology must not drift, merge, or collapse.
axis_rubric.physical_plausibility: Motion must respect rigid-body coupling, load direction, contact, reachable poses, and classical mechanics.
axis_rubric.temporal_consistency: The same mechanism and parts must persist without flicker, detachment, identity swap, or soft-body melting.

industrial_logic_questions.text: Does the video preserve the industrial cause-and-effect chain for this scenario: forklift overspeed during a tight turn causing pallet cargo to slide outward under centrifugal force?
Does any core equipment, person, vehicle, defect region, or emergency state change role without a visible causal trigger?
Does the generated event remain consistent with the domain context of visual security and violation monitoring and the task category rigid-body kinematics and coupled mechanism motion?

samples.video_generation_prompt: Use the provided reference image as the first frame and visual anchor. Generate a 5-8 second realistic industrial video. Scene: forklift overspeed during a tight turn causing pallet cargo to slide outward under centrifugal force. Reference subject: veh 014. Camera: perform a smooth constant-radius orbit around the subject; target motion value 45.0. Action: Show the rigid mechanism moving under load with all links, joints, tools, supports, and contact points staying physically coupled. Keep the same equipment identity, layout, colors, materials, background, and perspective cues from the reference image. Do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people. Avoid melting, flicker, identity swaps, component-count changes, impossible floating loads, rigid-body bending, and accidental global scene changes.

Does the generated event remain consistent with the domain context of visual security and violation monitoring and the task category topology mutation and structural failure?

samples.video_generation_prompt: Use the provided reference image as the first frame and visual anchor. Generate a 5-8 second realistic industrial video. Scene: factory perimeter wire fence broken by external impact with a visible gap in the restricted boundary. Reference subject: aerial work platform boom. Camera: perform a controlled dolly-in toward the local defect or failure region; target motion value 1.5. Action: Generate only the requested local break, short, crack, missing tooth, rope deformation, or structural failure; keep every unaffected area stable. Keep the same equipment identity, layout, colors, materials, background, and perspective cues from the reference image. Do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people. Avoid melting, flicker, identity swaps, component-count changes, impossible floating loads, rigid-body bending, and accidental global scene changes.

samples.prompt: Task objective: visual security for topology mutation and failure. Core scenario: factory perimeter wire fence broken by external impact with a visible gap in the restricted boundary. Reference subject: formwork climbing system. Motion requirement / viewpoint motion fidelity: perform a controlled dolly-in toward the local defect or failure region; target motion value 1.5. Industrial logic and fact alignment check: preserve the causal chain, equipment roles, personnel or vehicle states, compliance triggers, alarms, stops, and consequences described by the scenario. Geometric integrity check: preserve topology, rigid joints, load-bearing members, local defect boundaries, repeated structures, component counts, and spatial relationships; only the requested failure or defect region may change. Physical plausibility check: obey gravity, contact, rigid-body coupling, load paths, pressure direction, fluid diffusion, heat or flame propagation, and feasible emergency dynamics. Temporal consistency check: maintain object identity, material state, background, local event state, and cause-effect continuity across all frames without flicker, melting, or role switching. Reference and motion fidelity check: execute the requested viewpoint control while locking the reference identity, perspective, non-mutated regions, and background details. Execution constraints: do not add or remove functional parts; do not change component counts; do not merge separate structures unless the requested failure requires it; do not bend rigid members; do not replace the industrial scene with a different object. Scoring emphasis: geometric_integrity=1.80, industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment=1.10, physical_plausibility=1.05, reference_and_motion_fidelity=1.35, temporal_consistency=1.25.

axis_rubric.temporal_consistency: The defect must persist and evolve continuously without global scene regeneration.

samples.video_generation_prompt: Use the provided reference image as the first frame and visual anchor. Generate a 5-8 second realistic industrial video. Scene: unknown chemical liquid leaking and spreading across a dangerous-goods loading zone. Reference subject: solvent extraction battery. Camera: perform a controlled lateral pan that follows the evolving leak, spray, smoke, flame, or surge; target motion value 45.0. Action: Animate the fluid, smoke, flame, spray, mud, heat, or pressure-driven flow from the correct source and in the correct direction. Keep the same equipment identity, layout, colors, materials, background, and perspective cues from the reference image. Do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people. Avoid melting, flicker, identity swaps, component-count changes, impossible floating loads, rigid-body bending, and accidental global scene changes.

samples.prompt: Task objective: visual security for fluid dynamics and thermodynamics. Core scenario: unknown chemical liquid leaking and spreading across a dangerous-goods loading zone. Reference subject: piping manifold. Motion requirement / viewpoint motion fidelity: perform a controlled lateral pan that follows the evolving leak, spray, smoke, flame, or surge; target motion value 45.0. Industrial logic and fact alignment check: preserve the causal chain, equipment roles, personnel or vehicle states, compliance triggers, alarms, stops, and consequences described by the scenario. Geometric integrity check: preserve topology, rigid joints, load-bearing members, local defect boundaries, repeated structures, component counts, and spatial relationships; only the requested failure or defect region may change. Physical plausibility check: obey gravity, contact, rigid-body coupling, load paths, pressure direction, fluid diffusion, heat or flame propagation, and feasible emergency dynamics. Temporal consistency check: maintain object identity, material state, background, local event state, and cause-effect continuity across all frames without flicker, melting, or role switching. Reference and motion fidelity check: execute the requested viewpoint control while locking the reference identity, perspective, non-mutated regions, and background details. Execution constraints: do not add or remove functional parts; do not change component counts; do not merge separate structures unless the requested failure requires it; do not bend rigid members; do not replace the industrial scene with a different object. Scoring emphasis: geometric_integrity=1.05, industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment=1.25, physical_plausibility=1.85, reference_and_motion_fidelity=0.95, temporal_consistency=1.30.

Physical plausibility check: obey gravity, contact, rigid-body coupling, load paths, pressure direction, fluid diffusion, heat or flame propagation, and feasible emergency dynamics. Temporal consistency check: maintain object identity, material state, background, local event state, and cause-effect continuity across all frames without flicker, melting, or role switching. Reference and motion fidelity check: execute the requested viewpoint control while locking the reference identity, perspective, non-mutated regions, and background details. Execution constraints: do not add or remove functional parts; do not change component counts; do not merge separate structures unless the requested failure requires it; do not bend rigid members; do not replace the industrial scene with a different object. Scoring emphasis: geometric_integrity=1.35, industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment=1.00, physical_plausibility=1.00, reference_and_motion_fidelity=1.75, temporal_consistency=1.25.

axis_rubric.physical_plausibility: Motion must respect rigid-body coupling, load direction, contact, reachable poses, and classical mechanics.
axis_rubric.temporal_consistency: The same mechanism and parts must persist without flicker, detachment, identity swap, or soft-body melting.

industrial_logic_questions.text: Does the video preserve the industrial cause-and-effect chain for this scenario: multi-axis robotic arm performing high-precision grasping with tool-environment contact?
Does any core equipment, person, vehicle, defect region, or emergency state change role without a visible causal trigger?
Does the generated event remain consistent with the domain context of embodied intelligence and robotic operation and the task category rigid-body kinematics and coupled mechanism motion?

samples.video_generation_prompt: Use the provided reference image as the first frame and visual anchor. Generate a 5-8 second realistic industrial video. Scene: multi-axis robotic arm performing high-precision grasping with tool-environment contact. Reference subject: parallel kinematic hexapod. Camera: perform a smooth constant-radius orbit around the subject; target motion value 45.0. Action: Show the rigid mechanism moving under load with all links, joints, tools, supports, and contact points staying physically coupled. Keep the same equipment identity, layout, colors, materials, background, and perspective cues from the reference image. Do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people. Avoid melting, flicker, identity swaps, component-count changes, impossible floating loads, rigid-body bending, and accidental global scene changes.

samples.prompt: Task objective: embodied robotics for rigid body kinematics and coupling. Core scenario: multi-axis robotic arm performing high-precision grasping with tool-environment contact. Reference subject: surgical robot instrument arm. Motion requirement / viewpoint motion fidelity: perform a smooth constant-radius orbit around the subject; target motion value 45.0. Industrial logic and fact alignment check: preserve the causal chain, equipment roles, personnel or vehicle states, compliance triggers, alarms, stops, and consequences described by the scenario. Geometric integrity check: preserve topology, rigid joints, load-bearing members, local defect boundaries, repeated structures, component counts, and spatial relationships; only the requested failure or defect region may change. Physical plausibility check: obey gravity, contact, rigid-body coupling, load paths, pressure direction, fluid diffusion, heat or flame propagation, and feasible emergency dynamics. Temporal consistency check: maintain object identity, material state, background, local event state, and cause-effect continuity across all frames without flicker, melting, or role switching. Reference and motion fidelity check: execute the requested viewpoint control while locking the reference identity, perspective, non-mutated regions, and background details. Execution constraints: do not add or remove functional parts; do not change component counts; do not merge separate structures unless the requested failure requires it; do not bend rigid members; do not replace the industrial scene with a different object. Scoring emphasis: geometric_integrity=1.70, industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment=1.10, physical_plausibility=1.45, reference_and_motion_fidelity=0.85, temporal_consistency=1.25.

constraint_annotations.domain_scenario: multi-axis robotic arm performing high-precision grasping with tool-environment contact

sensitivity_variants.prompt_delta: Speed up the orbit by 40%, testing temporal consistency

samples.video_generation_prompt: Use the provided reference image as the first frame and visual anchor. Generate a 5-8 second realistic industrial video. Scene: multi-axis robotic arm performing high-precision grasping with tool-environment contact. Reference subject: parallel kinematic hexapod. Camera: perform a smooth constant-radius orbit around the subject; target motion value 45.0. Action: Show the rigid mechanism moving under load with all links, joints, tools, supports, and contact points staying physically coupled. Keep the same equipment identity, layout, colors, materials, background, and perspective cues from the reference image. Do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people. Avoid melting, flicker, identity swaps, component-count changes, impossible floating loads, rigid-body bending, and accidental global scene changes.

samples.prompt: Task objective: embodied robotics for rigid body kinematics and coupling. Core scenario: multi-axis robotic arm performing high-precision grasping with tool-environment contact. Reference subject: surgical robot instrument arm. Motion requirement / viewpoint motion fidelity: perform a smooth constant-radius orbit around the subject; target motion value 45.0. Industrial logic and fact alignment check: preserve the causal chain, equipment roles, personnel or vehicle states, compliance triggers, alarms, stops, and consequences described by the scenario. Geometric integrity check: preserve topology, rigid joints, load-bearing members, local defect boundaries, repeated structures, component counts, and spatial relationships; only the requested failure or defect region may change. Physical plausibility check: obey gravity, contact, rigid-body coupling, load paths, pressure direction, fluid diffusion, heat or flame propagation, and feasible emergency dynamics. Temporal consistency check: maintain object identity, material state, background, local event state, and cause-effect continuity across all frames without flicker, melting, or role switching. Reference and motion fidelity check: execute the requested viewpoint control while locking the reference identity, perspective, non-mutated regions, and background details. Execution constraints: do not add or remove functional parts; do not change component counts; do not merge separate structures unless the requested failure requires it; do not bend rigid members; do not replace the industrial scene with a different object. Scoring emphasis: geometric_integrity=1.70, industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment=1.10, physical_plausibility=1.45, reference_and_motion_fidelity=0.85, temporal_consistency=1.25.

Does any core equipment, person, vehicle, defect region, or emergency state change role without a visible causal trigger?
Does the generated event remain consistent with the domain context of embodied intelligence and robotic operation and the task category industrial logic and compliance execution?

samples.video_generation_prompt: Use the provided reference image as the first frame and visual anchor. Generate a 5-8 second realistic industrial video. Scene: worker approaching an automated line and triggering a light curtain that forces emergency braking. Reference subject: assembly line. Camera: hold a fixed monitoring view; no camera movement; the scene state must still evolve according to the safety or emergency trigger. Action: Show the violation or trigger, then the correct industrial consequence such as alarm, stop, braking, evacuation, or emergency escalation. Keep the same equipment identity, layout, colors, materials, background, and perspective cues from the reference image. Do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people. Avoid melting, flicker, identity swaps, component-count changes, impossible floating loads, rigid-body bending, and accidental global scene changes.

samples.prompt: Task objective: embodied robotics for industrial logic and compliance. Core scenario: worker approaching an automated line and triggering a light curtain that forces emergency braking. Reference subject: cobot assembly station. Motion requirement / viewpoint motion fidelity: hold a fixed monitoring view; no camera movement; the scene state must still evolve according to the safety or emergency trigger. Industrial logic and fact alignment check: preserve the causal chain, equipment roles, personnel or vehicle states, compliance triggers, alarms, stops, and consequences described by the scenario. Geometric integrity check: preserve topology, rigid joints, load-bearing members, local defect boundaries, repeated structures, component counts, and spatial relationships; only the requested failure or defect region may change. Physical plausibility check: obey gravity, contact, rigid-body coupling, load paths, pressure direction, fluid diffusion, heat or flame propagation, and feasible emergency dynamics. Temporal consistency check: maintain object identity, material state, background, local event state, and cause-effect continuity across all frames without flicker, melting, or role switching. Reference and motion fidelity check: execute the requested viewpoint control while locking the reference identity, perspective, non-mutated regions, and background details. Execution constraints: do not add or remove functional parts; do not change component counts; do not merge separate structures unless the requested failure requires it; do not bend rigid members; do not replace the industrial scene with a different object. Scoring emphasis: geometric_integrity=1.05, industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment=1.85, physical_plausibility=1.25, reference_and_motion_fidelity=0.95, temporal_consistency=1.35.

samples.application_value: Rigid mechanisms, robotic arms, cranes, CNC machines, excavators, and multi-link equipment where coupled motion must stay feasible.

axis_rubric.geometric_integrity: Highest priority: links, rigid joints, axes, supports, and spatial topology must not drift, merge, or collapse.
axis_rubric.physical_plausibility: Motion must respect rigid-body coupling, load direction, contact, reachable poses, and classical mechanics.
axis_rubric.temporal_consistency: The same mechanism and parts must persist without flicker, detachment, identity swap, or soft-body melting.

industrial_logic_questions.text: Does the video preserve the industrial cause-and-effect chain for this scenario: excavator multi-link loading motion or synchronized crawler-crane hoisting with visible load path?
Does any core equipment, person, vehicle, defect region, or emergency state change role without a visible causal trigger?
Does the generated event remain consistent with the domain context of construction engineering and heavy-load operation and the task category rigid-body kinematics and coupled mechanism motion?

samples.video_generation_prompt: Use the provided reference image as the first frame and visual anchor. Generate a 5-8 second realistic industrial video. Scene: excavator multi-link loading motion or synchronized crawler-crane hoisting with visible load path. Reference subject: excavator. Camera: perform a smooth constant-radius orbit around the subject; target motion value 45.0. Action: Show the rigid mechanism moving under load with all links, joints, tools, supports, and contact points staying physically coupled. Keep the same equipment identity, layout, colors, materials, background, and perspective cues from the reference image. Do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people. Avoid melting, flicker, identity swaps, component-count changes, impossible floating loads, rigid-body bending, and accidental global scene changes.

samples.prompt: Task objective: heavy load construction for topology mutation and failure. Core scenario: overloaded crawler crane causing wire-rope extreme deformation and possible snapping. Reference subject: lattice boom crawler crane. Motion requirement / viewpoint motion fidelity: perform a controlled dolly-in toward the local defect or failure region; target motion value 1.5. Industrial logic and fact alignment check: preserve the causal chain, equipment roles, personnel or vehicle states, compliance triggers, alarms, stops, and consequences described by the scenario. Geometric integrity check: preserve topology, rigid joints, load-bearing members, local defect boundaries, repeated structures, component counts, and spatial relationships; only the requested failure or defect region may change. Physical plausibility check: obey gravity, contact, rigid-body coupling, load paths, pressure direction, fluid diffusion, heat or flame propagation, and feasible emergency dynamics. Temporal consistency check: maintain object identity, material state, background, local event state, and cause-effect continuity across all frames without flicker, melting, or role switching. Reference and motion fidelity check: execute the requested viewpoint control while locking the reference identity, perspective, non-mutated regions, and background details. Execution constraints: do not add or remove functional parts; do not change component counts; do not merge separate structures unless the requested failure requires it; do not bend rigid members; do not replace the industrial scene with a different object. Scoring emphasis: geometric_integrity=1.80, industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment=1.10, physical_plausibility=1.05, reference_and_motion_fidelity=1.35, temporal_consistency=1.25.

Does the generated event remain consistent with the domain context of construction engineering and heavy-load operation and the task category fluid dynamics and thermodynamic evolution?

samples.video_generation_prompt: Use the provided reference image as the first frame and visual anchor. Generate a 5-8 second realistic industrial video. Scene: underground water pipe accidentally broken at a construction site causing muddy water to surge upward. Reference subject: thickener tank farm. Camera: perform a controlled lateral pan that follows the evolving leak, spray, smoke, flame, or surge; target motion value 45.0. Action: Animate the fluid, smoke, flame, spray, mud, heat, or pressure-driven flow from the correct source and in the correct direction. Keep the same equipment identity, layout, colors, materials, background, and perspective cues from the reference image. Do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people. Avoid melting, flicker, identity swaps, component-count changes, impossible floating loads, rigid-body bending, and accidental global scene changes.

samples.prompt: Task objective: heavy load construction for fluid dynamics and thermodynamics. Core scenario: underground water pipe accidentally broken at a construction site causing muddy water to surge upward. Reference subject: trenchless pipe jacking machine. Motion requirement / viewpoint motion fidelity: perform a controlled lateral pan that follows the evolving leak, spray, smoke, flame, or surge; target motion value 45.0. Industrial logic and fact alignment check: preserve the causal chain, equipment roles, personnel or vehicle states, compliance triggers, alarms, stops, and consequences described by the scenario. Geometric integrity check: preserve topology, rigid joints, load-bearing members, local defect boundaries, repeated structures, component counts, and spatial relationships; only the requested failure or defect region may change. Physical plausibility check: obey gravity, contact, rigid-body coupling, load paths, pressure direction, fluid diffusion, heat or flame propagation, and feasible emergency dynamics. Temporal consistency check: maintain object identity, material state, background, local event state, and cause-effect continuity across all frames without flicker, melting, or role switching. Reference and motion fidelity check: execute the requested viewpoint control while locking the reference identity, perspective, non-mutated regions, and background details. Execution constraints: do not add or remove functional parts; do not change component counts; do not merge separate structures unless the requested failure requires it; do not bend rigid members; do not replace the industrial scene with a different object. Scoring emphasis: geometric_integrity=1.05, industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment=1.25, physical_plausibility=1.85, reference_and_motion_fidelity=0.95, temporal_consistency=1.30.

Does the generated event remain consistent with the domain context of construction engineering and heavy-load operation and the task category spatial exploration and viewpoint control?

samples.video_generation_prompt: Use the provided reference image as the first frame and visual anchor. Generate a 5-8 second realistic industrial video. Scene: drone orbiting a hundred-ton bridge precast segment to inspect hoisting alignment. Reference subject: container terminal quay crane. Camera: perform a smooth constant-radius orbit around the subject; target motion value 75.0. Action: Use the camera motion to reveal spatial structure, hidden geometry, blind spots, or internal surfaces while preserving the reference identity. Keep the same equipment identity, layout, colors, materials, background, and perspective cues from the reference image. Do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people. Avoid melting, flicker, identity swaps, component-count changes, impossible floating loads, rigid-body bending, and accidental global scene changes.

samples.prompt: Task objective: heavy load construction for spatial exploration and viewpoint. Core scenario: drone orbiting a hundred-ton bridge precast segment to inspect hoisting alignment. Reference subject: cable stayed bridge deck segment. Motion requirement / viewpoint motion fidelity: perform a smooth constant-radius orbit around the subject; target motion value 75.0. Industrial logic and fact alignment check: preserve the causal chain, equipment roles, personnel or vehicle states, compliance triggers, alarms, stops, and consequences described by the scenario. Geometric integrity check: preserve topology, rigid joints, load-bearing members, local defect boundaries, repeated structures, component counts, and spatial relationships; only the requested failure or defect region may change. Physical plausibility check: obey gravity, contact, rigid-body coupling, load paths, pressure direction, fluid diffusion, heat or flame propagation, and feasible emergency dynamics. Temporal consistency check: maintain object identity, material state, background, local event state, and cause-effect continuity across all frames without flicker, melting, or role switching. Reference and motion fidelity check: execute the requested viewpoint control while locking the reference identity, perspective, non-mutated regions, and background details. Execution constraints: do not add or remove functional parts; do not change component counts; do not merge separate structures unless the requested failure requires it; do not bend rigid members; do not replace the industrial scene with a different object. Scoring emphasis: geometric_integrity=1.35, industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment=1.00, physical_plausibility=1.00, reference_and_motion_fidelity=1.75, temporal_consistency=1.25.

industrial_logic_questions.text: Does the video preserve the industrial cause-and-effect chain for this scenario: CNC machine performing multi-axis coupled cutting on a complex curved surface?
Does any core equipment, person, vehicle, defect region, or emergency state change role without a visible causal trigger?
Does the generated event remain consistent with the domain context of precision manufacturing and defect generation and the task category rigid-body kinematics and coupled mechanism motion?

samples.video_generation_prompt: Use the provided reference image as the first frame and visual anchor. Generate a 5-8 second realistic industrial video. Scene: CNC machine performing multi-axis coupled cutting on a complex curved surface. Reference subject: cnc machine. Camera: perform a smooth constant-radius orbit around the subject; target motion value 45.0. Action: Show the rigid mechanism moving under load with all links, joints, tools, supports, and contact points staying physically coupled. Keep the same equipment identity, layout, colors, materials, background, and perspective cues from the reference image. Do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people. Avoid melting, flicker, identity swaps, component-count changes, impossible floating loads, rigid-body bending, and accidental global scene changes.

samples.prompt: Task objective: precision defect gen for rigid body kinematics and coupling. Core scenario: CNC machine performing multi-axis coupled cutting on a complex curved surface. Reference subject: five axis machining center. Motion requirement / viewpoint motion fidelity: perform a smooth constant-radius orbit around the subject; target motion value 45.0. Industrial logic and fact alignment check: preserve the causal chain, equipment roles, personnel or vehicle states, compliance triggers, alarms, stops, and consequences described by the scenario. Geometric integrity check: preserve topology, rigid joints, load-bearing members, local defect boundaries, repeated structures, component counts, and spatial relationships; only the requested failure or defect region may change. Physical plausibility check: obey gravity, contact, rigid-body coupling, load paths, pressure direction, fluid diffusion, heat or flame propagation, and feasible emergency dynamics. Temporal consistency check: maintain object identity, material state, background, local event state, and cause-effect continuity across all frames without flicker, melting, or role switching. Reference and motion fidelity check: execute the requested viewpoint control while locking the reference identity, perspective, non-mutated regions, and background details. Execution constraints: do not add or remove functional parts; do not change component counts; do not merge separate structures unless the requested failure requires it; do not bend rigid members; do not replace the industrial scene with a different object. Scoring emphasis: geometric_integrity=1.70, industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment=1.10, physical_plausibility=1.45, reference_and_motion_fidelity=0.85, temporal_consistency=1.25.

Industrial logic and fact alignment check: preserve the causal chain, equipment roles, personnel or vehicle states, compliance triggers, alarms, stops, and consequences described by the scenario. Geometric integrity check: preserve topology, rigid joints, load-bearing members, local defect boundaries, repeated structures, component counts, and spatial relationships; only the requested failure or defect region may change. Physical plausibility check: obey gravity, contact, rigid-body coupling, load paths, pressure direction, fluid diffusion, heat or flame propagation, and feasible emergency dynamics. Temporal consistency check: maintain object identity, material state, background, local event state, and cause-effect continuity across all frames without flicker, melting, or role switching. Reference and motion fidelity check: execute the requested viewpoint control while locking the reference identity, perspective, non-mutated regions, and background details. Execution constraints: do not add or remove functional parts; do not change component counts; do not merge separate structures unless the requested failure requires it; do not bend rigid members; do not replace the industrial scene with a different object. Scoring emphasis: geometric_integrity=1.70, industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment=1.10, physical_plausibility=1.45, reference_and_motion_fidelity=0.85, temporal_consistency=1.25.

constraint_annotations.domain_scenario: CNC machine performing multi-axis coupled cutting on a complex curved surface

sensitivity_variants.prompt_delta: Increase camera orbit speed by 40% while maintaining the same angular trajectory
Add a slight vertical drift component to the orbit, rising 3 degrees over the sequence

samples.application_value: Rigid mechanisms, robotic arms, cranes, CNC machines, excavators, and multi-link equipment where coupled motion must stay feasible.

axis_rubric.geometric_integrity: Highest priority: links, rigid joints, axes, supports, and spatial topology must not drift, merge, or collapse.
axis_rubric.physical_plausibility: Motion must respect rigid-body coupling, load direction, contact, reachable poses, and classical mechanics.
axis_rubric.temporal_consistency: The same mechanism and parts must persist without flicker, detachment, identity swap, or soft-body melting.

Does the generated event remain consistent with the domain context of precision manufacturing and defect generation and the task category rigid-body kinematics and coupled mechanism motion?

samples.video_generation_prompt: Use the provided reference image as the first frame and visual anchor. Generate a 5-8 second realistic industrial video. Scene: dense PCB traces forming solder-bridge short circuits, missing gear teeth, or severe gear wear. Reference subject: gear hobbing machine. Camera: perform a controlled dolly-in toward the local defect or failure region; target motion value 1.5. Action: Generate only the requested local break, short, crack, missing tooth, rope deformation, or structural failure; keep every unaffected area stable. Keep the same equipment identity, layout, colors, materials, background, and perspective cues from the reference image. Do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people. Avoid melting, flicker, identity swaps, component-count changes, impossible floating loads, rigid-body bending, and accidental global scene changes.

samples.prompt: Task objective: precision defect gen for topology mutation and failure. Core scenario: dense PCB traces forming solder-bridge short circuits, missing gear teeth, or severe gear wear. Reference subject: pcb circuit board. Motion requirement / viewpoint motion fidelity: perform a controlled dolly-in toward the local defect or failure region; target motion value 1.5. Industrial logic and fact alignment check: preserve the causal chain, equipment roles, personnel or vehicle states, compliance triggers, alarms, stops, and consequences described by the scenario. Geometric integrity check: preserve topology, rigid joints, load-bearing members, local defect boundaries, repeated structures, component counts, and spatial relationships; only the requested failure or defect region may change. Physical plausibility check: obey gravity, contact, rigid-body coupling, load paths, pressure direction, fluid diffusion, heat or flame propagation, and feasible emergency dynamics. Temporal consistency check: maintain object identity, material state, background, local event state, and cause-effect continuity across all frames without flicker, melting, or role switching. Reference and motion fidelity check: execute the requested viewpoint control while locking the reference identity, perspective, non-mutated regions, and background details. Execution constraints: do not add or remove functional parts; do not change component counts; do not merge separate structures unless the requested failure requires it; do not bend rigid members; do not replace the industrial scene with a different object. Scoring emphasis: geometric_integrity=1.80, industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment=1.10, physical_plausibility=1.05, reference_and_motion_fidelity=1.35, temporal_consistency=1.25.

industrial_logic_questions.text: Does the video preserve the industrial cause-and-effect chain for this scenario: endoscope moving through a complex tube bundle while preserving pipe-wall geometry?
Does any core equipment, person, vehicle, defect region, or emergency state change role without a visible causal trigger?
Does the generated event remain consistent with the domain context of precision manufacturing and defect generation and the task category spatial exploration and viewpoint control?

samples.video_generation_prompt: Use the provided reference image as the first frame and visual anchor. Generate a 5-8 second realistic industrial video. Scene: endoscope moving through a complex tube bundle while preserving pipe-wall geometry. Reference subject: gas turbine compressor section. Camera: perform a smooth constant-radius orbit around the subject; target motion value 75.0. Action: Use the camera motion to reveal spatial structure, hidden geometry, blind spots, or internal surfaces while preserving the reference identity. Keep the same equipment identity, layout, colors, materials, background, and perspective cues from the reference image. Do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people. Avoid melting, flicker, identity swaps, component-count changes, impossible floating loads, rigid-body bending, and accidental global scene changes.

samples.prompt: Task objective: precision defect gen for spatial exploration and viewpoint. Core scenario: endoscope moving through a complex tube bundle while preserving pipe-wall geometry. Reference subject: heat exchanger bundle. Motion requirement / viewpoint motion fidelity: perform a smooth constant-radius orbit around the subject; target motion value 75.0. Industrial logic and fact alignment check: preserve the causal chain, equipment roles, personnel or vehicle states, compliance triggers, alarms, stops, and consequences described by the scenario. Geometric integrity check: preserve topology, rigid joints, load-bearing members, local defect boundaries, repeated structures, component counts, and spatial relationships; only the requested failure or defect region may change. Physical plausibility check: obey gravity, contact, rigid-body coupling, load paths, pressure direction, fluid diffusion, heat or flame propagation, and feasible emergency dynamics. Temporal consistency check: maintain object identity, material state, background, local event state, and cause-effect continuity across all frames without flicker, melting, or role switching. Reference and motion fidelity check: execute the requested viewpoint control while locking the reference identity, perspective, non-mutated regions, and background details. Execution constraints: do not add or remove functional parts; do not change component counts; do not merge separate structures unless the requested failure requires it; do not bend rigid members; do not replace the industrial scene with a different object. Scoring emphasis: geometric_integrity=1.35, industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment=1.00, physical_plausibility=1.00, reference_and_motion_fidelity=1.75, temporal_consistency=1.25.

axis_rubric.reference_and_motion_fidelity: Masked non-defect regions, background, and reference layout must remain locked while the defect evolves.

industrial_logic_questions.text: Does the video preserve the industrial cause-and-effect chain for this scenario: transmission tower overloaded by ice and snow until the load-bearing structure yields and collapses?
Does any core equipment, person, vehicle, defect region, or emergency state change role without a visible causal trigger?
Does the generated event remain consistent with the domain context of extreme-condition emergency response and the task category topology mutation and structural failure?

samples.video_generation_prompt: Use the provided reference image as the first frame and visual anchor. Generate a 5-8 second realistic industrial video. Scene: transmission tower overloaded by ice and snow until the load-bearing structure yields and collapses. Reference subject: wind turbine. Camera: perform a controlled dolly-in toward the local defect or failure region; target motion value 1.5. Action: Generate only the requested local break, short, crack, missing tooth, rope deformation, or structural failure; keep every unaffected area stable. Keep the same equipment identity, layout, colors, materials, background, and perspective cues from the reference image. Do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people. Avoid melting, flicker, identity swaps, component-count changes, impossible floating loads, rigid-body bending, and accidental global scene changes.

samples.prompt: Task objective: extreme emergency for topology mutation and failure. Core scenario: transmission tower overloaded by ice and snow until the load-bearing structure yields and collapses. Reference subject: tower crane luffing jib. Motion requirement / viewpoint motion fidelity: perform a controlled dolly-in toward the local defect or failure region; target motion value 1.5. Industrial logic and fact alignment check: preserve the causal chain, equipment roles, personnel or vehicle states, compliance triggers, alarms, stops, and consequences described by the scenario. Geometric integrity check: preserve topology, rigid joints, load-bearing members, local defect boundaries, repeated structures, component counts, and spatial relationships; only the requested failure or defect region may change. Physical plausibility check: obey gravity, contact, rigid-body coupling, load paths, pressure direction, fluid diffusion, heat or flame propagation, and feasible emergency dynamics. Temporal consistency check: maintain object identity, material state, background, local event state, and cause-effect continuity across all frames without flicker, melting, or role switching. Reference and motion fidelity check: execute the requested viewpoint control while locking the reference identity, perspective, non-mutated regions, and background details. Execution constraints: do not add or remove functional parts; do not change component counts; do not merge separate structures unless the requested failure requires it; do not bend rigid members; do not replace the industrial scene with a different object. Scoring emphasis: geometric_integrity=1.80, industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment=1.10, physical_plausibility=1.05, reference_and_motion_fidelity=1.35, temporal_consistency=1.25.
